plan: 1. Verify existing implementation: `src/domain/snapshotToScene.ts` already contains `mapSnapshotToScene(snapshot: NetworkSnapshot): SceneParams` â€” a pure, deterministic function that maps network conditions to 6 art-direction parameters (height, symmetry, fracture, fog, lightIntensity, ruinLevel), all clamped to [0,1].

2. Verify normalization pipeline: The mapper uses `normalizeRtt()` (0-1000ms â†’ 0-1), `normalizeJitter()` (0-500ms â†’ 0-1), and `clamp()` from `src/domain/normalize.ts`. Packet loss is already 0-1. A composite `degradation` signal blends RTT (55%), jitter (35%), and loss (15%).

3. Verify mapping formulas meet acceptance criteria: height = 1 - rttNorm (fast â†’ high), symmetry = 1 - (0.7Ă—jitter + 0.4Ă—rtt) (fast â†’ high symmetry), fracture = 0.7Ă—jitter + 0.3Ă—loss (poor â†’ high fracture), fog = degradation, lightIntensity = 1 - degradation, ruinLevel = 0.3Ă—rtt + 0.3Ă—jitter + 0.4Ă—loss.
4. Verify domain purity: `src/domain/snapshotToScene.ts` imports only from `./types` and `./normalize` â€” no three.js or side effects. Already validated by `tests/unit/domain/types.test.ts`.

6. Verify unit tests: `tests/unit/domain/snapshotToScene.test.ts` (166 lines) covers ideal conditions (high height/symmetry), degraded conditions (high fracture/ruinLevel), edge cases (empty samples, zeros, extremes), monotonicity (heightâ†“ as RTTâ†‘, fractureâ†‘ as jitterâ†‘, ruinLevelâ†‘ as lossâ†‘), and normalization bounds.

7. Verify preset integration: `tests/unit/domain/presets.test.ts` confirms fast preset yields height > 0.9 / fracture < 0.05, poor preset yields height < 0.1 / fracture > 0.7, validating end-to-end acceptance criteria.
8. Run full quality gate: `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e` â€” all must pass to close the ticket.
9. Mark ticket passes=true and update progress log.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run test` â€” all 218 tests pass including 13 test files covering snapshotToScene, normalize, presets, presetState, types, and aggregator.
Verify monotonicity tests: height decreases as RTT increases, fracture increases as jitter increases, ruinLevel increases as packet loss increases.
Verify boundary normalization: all SceneParams fields are in [0, 1] for edge cases (zeros, extreme values, NaN, Infinity).
Verify preset round-trip: fast preset â†’ mapSnapshotToScene â†’ height > 0.9, fracture < 0.05; poor preset â†’ height < 0.1, fracture > 0.7.
Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck` to confirm no type errors or lint violations.
Run `npm run test:e2e` for browser smoke tests (when applicable).
risks: The mapper currently ignores the `bandwidth` field from NetworkSnapshot â€” if downstream tickets (US-011, US-013) need bandwidth-driven parameters, the mapper may need extension.
Normalization ceilings are hardcoded (RTT max=1000ms, jitter max=500ms) â€” real-world conditions exceeding these caps will saturate at 1.0, losing granularity at extreme values.

The `symmetry` formula (1 - 0.7Ă—jitter - 0.4Ă—rtt) can theoretically sum weights > 1.0, but `clamp()` prevents out-of-range output â€” this is intentional but may compress the symmetry range for moderate conditions.
No ADR was written for the mapping weights/formulas â€” if art-direction tuning is needed later, the rationale for current weights (55/35/15 degradation blend) is only in code comments.
